mission.label: Our Mission
mission.title: Bridging Past & Present
mission.p1: The project combines cultural heritage, field data collection, and digital technology to preserve the invisible traces of architectural tradition.
mission.p2: Every stonemason mark tells a story - of the craftsman who carved it, the guild they belonged to, and the monument they helped build. Stone Mark brings these stories to light.

cta.title: Join Us in Preserving History
cta.desc: Be part of a global movement to document and protect the craft traditions that shaped our world
cta.footer: Open collaboration ensures the project remains transparent, reliable, and useful to both the public and the academic community.

partner.title: Our Institutional Partner
partner.subtitle: Portuguese Association for the Urban Rehabilitation and Protection of Heritage
partner.desc: APRUPP is a non-profit association dedicated to the study, protection, and promotion of urban heritage, with a special focus on the preservation of traditional construction techniques and the memory of the built environment.
partner.heritage_desc: Promoting the safeguarding and appreciation of architectural and cultural heritage.
partner.urban_desc: Supporting sustainable urban rehabilitation and the revitalization of historic centers.
partner.civic_desc: Encouraging community participation in the protection of heritage.
partner.knowledge_desc: Disseminating research and best practices in heritage conservation.

hero.label: About the Project
hero.title:
Preserving History,
One Mark at a Time.
hero.desc: Stone Mark is a web application dedicated to the documentation and exploration of stonemason marks — the unique symbols engraved by ancient craftsmen on monuments across history.

features.label: Key Features
features.title: Tools for Heritage Explorers
features.desc: Designed to make your journey through history seamless, engaging, and unforgettable

1.title: Capture & Submit Marks
1.desc: Photograph and submit authentic stonemason marks directly from the app, contributing to a growing digital archive of craftsmanship heritage.

2.title: Search Monuments
2.desc: Discover monuments and stonemason symbols through a fast and intuitive search experience.

3.title: Advanced Filtering
3.desc: Filter marks by monument to explore detailed cultural patterns and associations.

4.title: Bookmarks & Collections
4.desc: Save your favourite monuments or marks to personalized collections — perfect for research, teaching, or exploration.

5.title: Geolocation on Map
5.desc: Explore nearby stonemason marks and monuments in real-time using an interactive map with precise geolocation features.

6.title: Cultural Preservation
6.desc: Support the documentation and preservation of stonemason heritage through community participation and open historical data.

unesco.label: Global Initiative
unesco.title: Partnering with UNESCO
unesco.desc: Stone Mark is being developed in collaboration with UNESCO to preserve and promote world heritage sites. With proven technology and a scalable architecture, we're preparing for international expansion.
unesco.impact_desc: Empowering millions to connect with heritage
unesco.growth_desc: Built for scale with proven architecture

discover-hero-section.label: The Real Deal
discover-hero-section.title: Deciphering the Stone Code.
discover-hero-section.desc: Masons' marks are the unsung autographs of the architects of our past. StoneMark uses AI to document, group, and preserve these fading symbols for future generations.

1.label: Customization
1.title: History in the Details
1.desc: Stonecutters were paid by the piece. To ensure they got their due, they carved a personal symbol into every block they finished. These marks are personal, geometrical, and infinitely varied.

2.label: Preservation
2.title: Fighting Time
2.desc: Weather, pollution, and renovation are slowly erasing these marks. By creating a digital twin of every mark found, we ensure that even if the stone crumbles, the mason's signature remains.

discover-why-it-matters-section.label: The Significance
discover-why-it-matters-section.title: Why Mason's Marks Matter
discover-why-it-matters-section.desc: These simple carvings are a direct link to the individual artisans who built our shared heritage, offering insights into history, culture, and craftsmanship.

1.title: Signatures of the Builders
1.desc: Marks act as personal signatures, helping identify specific masons and how construction sites were organized, especially where written records are scarce.

2.title: Construction Clues
2.desc: Different symbols reveal distinct construction phases and the evolution of craftsmanship, allowing researchers to map a monument's history.

3.title: Traces of Cultural Exchange
3.desc: Similar marks found across vast distances suggest the migration of masons and the spread of shared knowledge, acting as archaeological fingerprints.

4.title: A Fragile Heritage
4.desc: Slowly disappearing due to erosion and pollution, these marks are a fragile heritage. Digital documentation is crucial to preserve them for future study.

1.title: Find a Mark
1.desc: Locate a mason's mark on a historical monument, cathedral wall, or ancient bridge structure.

2.title: Capture
2.desc: Take a high-contrast photo using your telephone camera. Good lighting helps reveal faint carvings.

3.title: AI Analysis
3.desc: Our embeddings model analyzes the geometry, grouping similar marks across different locations.

4.title: Heritage
4.desc: The mark is indexed into the global public database, accessible to researchers worldwide.

discover-contribution-section.title: Contributing to the Project
discover-contribution-section.desc: StoneMark is an open, academic-minded initiative. For those with technical, design, or research experience, contributions to the platform itself are welcome.
discover-contribution-section.footer: Open collaboration ensures the project remains transparent, reliable, and useful to both the public and the academic community.

discover-inter-marks-section.label: Methodology
discover-inter-marks-section.title: Interpreting Mason’s Marks
discover-inter-marks-section.desc: An overview of how specialists understand and classify these marks, offering a deeper insight into their significance.

types.personal_desc: The most common type, used to identify a mason's work for payment and quality control.
types.assembly_desc: Practical instructions like 'this side up' or numbering for sequential placement.
types.symbolic_desc: Rarer marks with ritual functions, believed to ward off evil or mark sacred boundaries.

discover-inter-marks-section.carving_desc: How a mark was carved provides clues about its period and maker. The depth, angle, and rhythm of the chisel strokes are all valuable data points for analysis.

carving.point_desc: Produces small circular depressions, common in late medieval periods.
carving.flat_desc: Creates clean linear incisions, used for quick tally signatures.

discover-inter-marks-section.arch_desc: When studied collectively, mason's marks serve as micro-historical evidence, allowing researchers to reconstruct construction phases and map the movement of craftsmen.

arch.phases_desc: Clusters of identical marks can indicate a specific period of work or later repairs using different teams.
arch.map_desc: Similar symbols appearing across distant sites help trace medieval mobility and the transmission of architectural knowledge.

discover-inter-marks-section.challenges_desc: Context is everything. The same simple symbol could be used by many masons, and weathering can alter the original shape, making interpretation a complex puzzle.
discover-inter-marks-section.human_desc: Ultimately, interpreting these marks transforms stone walls into documents of lived history. They reveal the identities, movements, and techniques of artisans whose names were never recorded, bringing us closer to the human story behind our greatest monuments.
